data.title: Parkland shareholder Engine Capital to vote against Sunoco takeover
data.description:
Engine Capital told the board of Canadian fuel distributor Parkland (OTCPK:PKIUF) on Friday it plans to vote against the company’s $9.1B cash and stock takeover by Sunoco LP (NYSE:SUN), saying the offer is inadequate and the sale process was "expedited and flawed."

"Our opposition to this transaction is directed at its terms - not at Sunoco or its management team," according to the letter from Engine Capital, which owns ~2.5% of Parkland (OTCPK:PKIUF). "Unless shareholders act collectively to vote down this transaction, the company will be sold in an inadequate deal that was hastily negotiated by a conflicted and lame duck board."

The activist investor said a sum of the parts methodology is most appropriate to value Parkland (OTCPK:PKIUF), and that the average sum of the parts valuation by sell-side analysts points to a valuation of $52.50/share.

The proposed deal came as Parkland's (OTCPK:PKIUF) largest shareholder, Simpson Oil, appeared on the verge of gaining control of the Canadian company's board; longtime Parkland CEO Bob Espey announced in April that he planned to step down after months of pressure from Simpson.

Sunoco LP's (NYSE:SUN) general partner is owned by Energy Transfe (ET).

data.title: Apellis phase 3 Empaveli data demonstrates sustained proteinuria reduction
data.description:
• New phase 3 data on Apellis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:APLS) and Sobi's Empaveli (pegcetacoplan) indicated that the treatment led to proteinuria reduction that was sustained for a year. 
  • At week 26, there was a 68% proteinuria reduction for those on Empaveli compared to placebo, which was sustained at one year.
  • Those on the drug also saw kidney function stabilization based on estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR).
  • The VALIANT study is exploring the treatment for C3 glomerulopathy (C3G) and primary immune complex membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis (IC-MPGN).
  • Empaveli is currently approved for paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria.

data.title: Lululemon knocked off balance amid tariff turbulance and margin squeeze
data.description:
Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) limped into Friday’s open down as much as 20% as the company’s second quarter and FY25 outlook undermined investors’ confidence that the company can thrive in a tariff-ladened environment given its heavy reliance on off-shore manufacturing.

Lululemon’s (NASDAQ:LULU) Q1 performance came in largely as expected with sales and profits both improving from the year ago quarter, beating expectations. But comparable sales were soft, especially in the Americas, as the company’s focus on expanding in China and product newness stoked concerns about a misalignment in priorities.

“Fixing the Americas should be the top priority,” says Jefferies Randal Konik, especially since it represents three-quarters of Lululemon’s business. “Yet the focus isn’t resonating broadly and the unaddressed rise in competition has led to increased promotions.”

But Lululemon's (NASDAQ:LULU) challenges extend beyond the Americas. CEO Calvin McDonald acknowledged on the company’s earnings call that the evolving tariff landscape is creating uncertainty across retail—just as consumers are beginning to weigh how these pressures affect their everyday spending. That shift could push Lululemon’s premium-priced athleisure into markdown territory.

While promotional pricing is being considered as a strategy to reignite sales in the Americas, CEO McDonald raised the possibility that the company would raise prices to offset tariff headwinds.  

“When we think about the tariff impact to mitigation actions, one would be pricing. We are planning to take strategic price increases looking item by item across our assortment as we typically do, and it will be price increases on a small portion of our assortment, and they will be modest in nature,” he said on the call.

Despite mitigation efforts, Lululemon is expected to bear the brunt of tariff pressures in Q2, as selective price hikes and supply chain improvements won’t take effect until later in the year, according to BTIG’s Janine Stichter. With limited tariff protection in the first half, Morgan Stanley’s Alex Stranton argues that LULU’s trimmed profit outlook supports the bear case—that its industry-leading margins may be unsustainable amid flat or declining comps in the Americas.

data.title: The U.S. economy is gradually slowing down despite tech-led market gains – JPM
data.description:
The U.S. economy is gradually slowing down, despite the S&P 500 (SP500) recently climbing back above 6,000 for the first time since February, according to David Kelly, J.P. Morgan Asset Management chief global strategist.

In an interview with CNBC, Kelly highlighted that the recent jobs report was “a lot softer than the headline suggested,” noting that “we saw over 600,000 jobs lost according to the household survey… that’s a negative signal.”

Kelly projects the U.S. economy is heading toward modest growth of around 1.5%, not the 3% that some have suggested.

“This is a 1.5% economy. This is not a 3% economy,” he stated, while explaining that job growth might average close to 100,000 going forward, though it “could bounce negative.”

Despite these warning signs, Kelly supported the Federal Reserve’s decision to hold off on rate cuts, citing ongoing inflation pressures. “The Fed has got a responsibility to control inflation,” he said, pointing to immigration pushing up wages and tariffs that have “not fed through to inflation yet, but will.”

Kelly attributed his conservative GDP growth projection to declining immigration and labor force constraints. While acknowledging that productivity will increase long-term due to AI, he emphasized that businesses need more incentives to invest.

“We need to continue to be the leaders in AI,” Kelly concluded, while suggesting that current conditions are causing “businesses to hesitate to invest rather than to go full bore ahead.”

More on the U.S. Economy:

  • Nonfarm payrolls growth slows in May, still it's a healthy gain
  • Fmr. St. Louis Fed Pres. Bullard: The Fed will hold until September
  • Fed's Schmid says tariffs will start feeding into prices in coming months
  • U.S. trade deficit narrows sharply in April as imports plunge
  • Weak Jobs And Rising Inflation Fuel Stagflation Fears

data.title: SuperCom gains on new contract with Southeastern electronic monitoring service provider
data.description:
• SuperCom (NASDAQ:SPCB) Friday said that it secured a new contract with an established Southeast-based service provider, sending shares up over +8% in morning trade.
  • The collaboration is expected to facilitate SuperCom's (NASDAQ:SPCB) entrance into additional U.S. states, including Florida and Mississippi, through the provider's established presence and further accelerate the Company's nationwide expansion momentum.
  • Through this partnership, SuperCom (SPCB) and this provider have already submitted a proposal to an RFP together.
  • "This marks our 9th new service provider partnership since summer 2024, further validating our technology and go-to-market strategy," SuperCom CEO Ordan Trabelsi said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Teva, Amneal rated Buy, Viatris Neutral as Goldman Sachs reviews generics space
data.description:
Goldman Sachs on Friday reviewed the generic drug industry, initiating the coverage of Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) and Amneal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMRX) with Buy ratings and Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS) with a Neutral rating.

The analyst Matt Dellatorre issued mostly positive remarks on the industry, citing opportunities to benefit from the sector's “undemanding” valuations driven by recent micro volatility and macro concerns.

“Broadly speaking, while the pace of legacy product erosion remains the key risk to near-term top-line growth and tariffs represent an overhang on margins (in our view, manageable), we are optimistic on the forward outlook,” Dellatorre wrote.

As reasons for the bullish outlook, the analyst cited multiple factors, including ongoing portfolio changes favoring higher-value products such as branded drugs and a stable pricing outlook for U.S. generics.

With a 12-month per-share target of $24 on Teva (NYSE:TEVA), the analyst pointed to an improvement in the Israeli drugmaker’s core business, which he said could generate upside to near-mid-term EBITDA consensus driven by strong growth in its branded portfolio.

“We see the strongest (above consensus) earnings’ growth potential for TEVA (where the branded portfolio now represents the key growth engine and a significant valuation lever) with recent clinical updates demonstrating the company’s ability to innovate,” Dellatorre wrote.

For Amneal (NASDAQ:AMRX), the analyst issued a 12-month target of $12, citing a positive growth outlook for the company’s core business due to its robust pipeline and track record of business execution.

With a 12-month target of $10 on Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS), Goldman Sachs argued that the FactSet consensus Pfizer (PFE) spinoff appears “fair” and noted a lack of clarity in the company’s growth outlook due to structural dynamics in its core business.

data.title: Fed's Bowman outlines agenda for bank regulatory reform, including SLR changes
data.description:
Michelle Bowman, the Federal Reserve's new vice chair for supervision, on Friday outlined plans to ease regulatory headwinds for banks, including potential changes to capital requirements and bank merger oversight.

Since the 2008 financial crisis, regulators have imposed a barrage of rules aimed at bolstering the banking system, though Bowman -- alongside bank executives -- are arguing that that accumulation warrants reconsideration. 

"Our goal should not be to prevent banks from failing or even eliminate the risk that they will. Our goal should be to make banks safe to fail, meaning that they can be allowed to fail without threatening to destabilize the rest of the banking system," she said in remarks prepared for delivery at the Psaros Center for Financial Markets and Policy event in Georgetown University, Washington, D.C.

Meanwhile, Bowman highlighted that financial market distortions can stem from over-calibrated capital rules, "disfavoring some activities over others in a way that is divorced from prudential safety and soundness goals and economic conditions."

She specifically pointed to leverage requirements, namely the enhanced supplementary leverage ratio (eSLR), which is applicable to the largest lenders. "When leverage ratios become the binding capital constraint at an excessive level, they can create market distortions."

That means "banks are less inclined to engage in low-risk activities like Treasury market intermediation and revise their business activities in a way that is neither justified nor responsive to their customer needs," Bowman added.

Regulators are expected to deliver a proposal for addressing the original calibration of eSLR, which "was based on forecasts of the level of reserves and other so-called 'safe assets' in the system that are now far out of line with current levels."

But that may not be enough to address issues in the broader regulatory capital framework, she said, noting the U.S. central bank will host an event in July to assess "whether capital requirements as currently structured and calibrated are operating as intended—in a complementary fashion."

The conference will include discussions on potential changes to leverage ratio requirements, stress testing, the GSIB surcharge and the Basel III capital requirements.

The top banking cop is also touched on banking applications, which she said should be revised, given processing delays, for example. "Streamlining the applications for de novo formation, and establishing clearer standards for approval, may encourage more de novo activity."

data.title: ABM reaffirms $3.65–$3.80 EPS guidance as record $1.1B bookings and segment growth drive outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ABM Industries Incorporated (ABM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Scott B. Salmirs, President and CEO, highlighted a return to organic growth in both Business & Industry (B&I) and Manufacturing & Distribution (M&D), improved cash flow, and record $1.1 billion in new bookings during the first half. "Overall, we posted 3.8% organic revenue growth, highlighted by continued recovery in our core commercial office markets, new contract wins and a diminished impact from prior year of client exits in M&D." He reported strong performances in Aviation and Education, noting temporary project delays in ATS but emphasizing market health and expectations for a strong year.
  • Salmirs stated, "We expect delayed projects from Q2 to resume in the third quarter and are reaffirming our full year adjusted EPS guidance."
  • He described segment highlights, including B&I's positive momentum due to improving prime office indicators, M&D's earlier-than-expected return to organic growth, new $190 million microgrid business, large Aviation contract wins, and $700 million Technical Solutions backlog. He added, "We believe our capabilities and our strategies to enhance them are fully in line with where demand is going."
  • Earl Ray Ellis, Executive VP & CFO, addressed a definitional update to non-GAAP financial measures after SEC communication, noting, "Importantly, there was no impact to our current quarter's results."
  • Ellis reported, "Revenue grew 4.6% year-over-year to $2.1 billion, driven by 3.8% organic growth and contributions from our 2024 acquisition of Quality Uptime Services."
  • Ellis added, "Free cash flow for the quarter was $15 million, an improvement of $138 million over quarter 1."

Outlook

  • ABM reaffirmed its full year adjusted EPS guidance of $3.65 to $3.80 and an adjusted EBITDA margin between 6.3% and 6.5%. Management expects normalized free cash flow for the full year in the range of $250 million to $290 million, excluding $30 million to $40 million of ELEVATE and integration costs and any portion of the RavenVolt earn-out payment.
  • Ellis stated, "We are maintaining our interest expense forecast of $80 million to $84 million and continue to expect a normalized tax rate before discrete items of 29% to 30%."
  • Management expects delayed Q2 projects to resume in Q3, supporting the outlook for both revenue and margin improvement in the second half.

Financial Results

  • ABM reported $2.1 billion in revenue for the quarter and adjusted EPS of $0.86. Net income was $42.2 million or $0.67 per diluted share. Adjusted net income was $54.1 million or $0.86 per diluted share.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $125.9 million and adjusted EBITDA margin was 6.2%.
  • B&I revenue reached $1 billion, with a 3% increase and a 40 basis point margin improvement. Aviation revenue was $260.1 million, up 9%, with operating profit up 26%. M&D generated $398.1 million in revenue, a 2% increase, while Education saw 1% revenue growth. Technical Solutions delivered 19% revenue growth to $210.2 million, with margin pressure from project mix and timing.
  • Available liquidity was $657.8 million, including $58.7 million in cash and cash equivalents. Total indebtedness was $1.6 billion.

Q&A

  • Timothy Michael Mulrooney, William Blair: Asked about the RavenVolt earn-out, cash flow bridge to the full year target, and B&I organic growth trajectory. Ellis explained the earn-out expectations and cash flow normalization, stating, "We believe that we're going to have incremental improvements in cash flow for both Q3 and Q4." Salmirs said, "We like to believe now we're in positive organic growth territory for B&I from here on out."
  • Andrew John Wittmann, Baird: Inquired about M&D's expanded service offerings and margin dynamics. Salmirs described the strategic move to higher-margin, stickier services in core client operations. Regarding margin, he noted investments in sales talent and strategic pricing rather than broad-based market competitiveness.
  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank: Asked about project delays in Technical Solutions and Education segment trends. Ellis clarified that project delays were timing-related and margins are expected to normalize. Salmirs described Education as "pretty normalized" with renewals and investment trends supporting a stable outlook.
  • Jasper James Bibb, Truist Securities: Queried about organic growth trajectory and details on battery energy storage contract. Salmirs highlighted a record $700 million backlog and ongoing client demand despite tariff and tax credit uncertainties.
  • Joshua K. Chan, UBS: Sought detail on ATS margin impacts from project delays and mix. Salmirs broke down the margin effect as related to project phase timing. Chan also asked about B&I market positioning, with Salmirs emphasizing ABM's scale, execution, and market share in prime office spaces.
  • Marc Frye Riddick, Sidoti: Asked about cash usage prioritization and M&A pipeline. Ellis described an active M&A environment with focus on deals that enhance ABM's strategic positioning.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on cash flow normalization, margin sustainability, and growth trajectory, expressing generally positive sentiment with detailed follow-ups on execution challenges and segment strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, frequently reaffirming guidance and emphasizing operational progress and market opportunities. Salmirs stated, "We're confident this investment will enhance our service delivery, improve operational efficiency and further elevate the client experience."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts displayed increased optimism, with less focus on execution risk related to ERP implementation and more emphasis on segment-specific growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained steady, with management reaffirming the $3.65 to $3.80 adjusted EPS range and free cash flow target. The major shift was the earlier-than-expected return to organic growth in both B&I and M&D, compared to expectations in the previous quarter for growth to resume later in the year.
  • There was increased discussion of segment expansion, especially in M&D and Technical Solutions, and a stronger tone regarding market recovery, particularly in prime office and Aviation.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from ERP and backlog concerns to more granular inquiries about segment-specific drivers and pipeline sustainability.
  • Management's confidence level increased, supported by record bookings and a positive cash flow trajectory.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited potential risks from project approval delays, working capital normalization, and possible changes to tax credits for energy projects, but indicated these are being managed with operational adjustments and ongoing client engagement.
  • Analysts asked about margin pressures in M&D and Technical Solutions, with management attributing these to strategic investments and project timing rather than systemic competitive threats.
  • The ERP implementation continues to be monitored, though progress has reduced operational friction.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that ABM's return to organic growth in key segments, record $1.1 billion in new bookings, and reaffirmed guidance for both EPS and free cash flow position the company for continued margin improvement and expansion. The focus remains on leveraging scale, integrated technology, and strategic investments to capture growth opportunities in core markets, while ongoing operational enhancements and a robust M&A pipeline support long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Westbridge Renewable launches normal course issuer bid, to buyback up to 5% of shares
data.description:
• Westbridge Renewable Energy (WEB: CA) (OTCQX:WEGYF) received TSXV approval to initiate a new normal course issuer bid (NCIB) starting June 9, 2025.
  • The program allows for the repurchase of up to 5.06M common shares, or ~5% of total outstanding shares.
  • All repurchased shares will be cancelled.
  • The NCIB will end on June 8, 2026, or earlier if the full amount is bought back.
  • The company plans to fund the NCIB with available cash.
  • In a prior NCIB (March 2024–March 2025), Westbridge repurchased and cancelled 693,800 shares.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: JBDI regains Nasdaq bid price compliance, addresses financial filing deficiency
data.description:
• JBDI Holdings (NASDAQ:JBDI) received Nasdaq confirmation on June 3, 2025, that it regained compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • On the same day, Nasdaq issued a deficiency notice due to JBDI’s delayed filing of its quarterly financials.
  • JBDI filed the required financials on June 5, 2025, and plans to inform Nasdaq to resolve the compliance issue.
  • Source: Press release
